title: Rubrics, Model Judges, Pairwise Comparison, Human Calibration, and Uncertainty · Language Clinic: Judge Variance
summary: A judge model sees model names, produces unexplained one-to-five scores, agrees with itself, replaces human review, and silently overrides deterministic safety failures. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the judge variance increment in calibrated mixed-method evaluation board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Measure repeatability, position effects, rubric sensitivity, reference leakage, adversarial manipulation, and judge-version drift.
Blind model, prompt, provider, and treatment identity; randomize order; use pairwise comparison when it improves judgment reliability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-rubrics-judge-calibration-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-rubrics-judge-calibration-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_rubrics_judge_calibration_3(rubric_cases: int = 60, blinded_cases: int = 60, human_calibration_cases: int = 18, disagreement_cases: int = 7, silent_overrides: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if rubric_cases < 30 or blinded_cases != rubric_cases:
        return False, blinded_cases, "rubric-blinding"
    if human_calibration_cases < 12:
        return False, human_calibration_cases, "human-calibration"
    if disagreement_cases < 1:
        return False, disagreement_cases, "judge-disagreement"
    if silent_overrides > 0:
        return False, silent_overrides, "silent-judge-override"
    assert blinded_cases == rubric_cases and silent_overrides == 0
    return True, human_calibration_cases, "calibrated-agent-judgment"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-judge-variance-vgmai8
# Competency: agentpy-judge-variance.
# Case 8301: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def evidence_agentpy_judge_variance_vgmai8(rubric_cases: int = 60, blinded_cases: int = 60, human_calibration_cases: int = 18, disagreement_cases: int = 7, silent_overrides: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vgmai8 = "vgmai8-8iamgv-8301"
    assert evidence_variant_vgmai8
    evidence_axes_vgmai8 = (
        "authorityvgmai8",
        "interaction8301",
        "tool8iamgv",
        "statevgmai88301",
        "evaluation8301vgmai8",
        "recovery8iamgv8301",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vgmai8)) == 6
    if rubric_cases < 30 or blinded_cases != rubric_cases:
        return False, blinded_cases, "rubric-blinding"
    if human_calibration_cases < 12:
        return False, human_calibration_cases, "human-calibration"
    if disagreement_cases < 1:
        return False, disagreement_cases, "judge-disagreement"
    if silent_overrides > 0:
        return False, silent_overrides, "silent-judge-override"
    assert blinded_cases == rubric_cases and silent_overrides == 0
    return True, human_calibration_cases, "calibrated-agent-judgment"
